title: Transitional Housing/Shelter * Native American Community - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Transitional Housing/Shelter * Native American Community 2 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - Disabilities Issues Ex-Offenders Native American Community AIDS/HIV Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF THE COOS, LOWER UMPQUA, AND SIUSLAW INDIANS GOVERNMENT OFFICE EMERGENCY ASSISTANCE/HOMELESS PREVENTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transitional housing rental assistance or a rental deposit, which may include placement in community-based crisis centers, transitional housing, or motels, or payment of one month of rent or a deposit toward a new rental unit. Assistance is based on funding availability and is not guaranteed. 1245 Fulton Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Must be enrolled in a federally recognized tribe, and families that are experiencing homelessness. Homelessness that was not caused by eviction for drug or alcohol issues, criminal activity, or any other reason due to the fault of the family, excluding non-payment of rent. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 888-1310 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NCH'I WANA HOUSING HOUSING ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with application fees, security deposits, move-in costs, and transitional housing in order to help individuals overcome barriers to establishing permanent housing. 2025 W 7th Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older and families with minor children who identify as Native American and are experiencing homelessness Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (541) 436-3810 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONFEDERATED TRIBES OF THE COOS, LOWER UMPQUA, AND SIUSLAW INDIANS EMERGENCY ASSISTANCE/HOMELESS PREVENTION NCH'I WANA HOUSING HOUSING ASSISTANCE
